case.title: Repeat ORC — Home Depot Power Tools, San Bernardino
case.summary: Three-person ORC crew targeting DeWalt and Milwaukee power tools. Push-out theft through lumber yard exit. Subjects have been identified at four Home Depot locations in San Bernardino County over the past three weeks. Cumulative loss estimated at $22,600. Recovered stolen merchandise being resold at Colton swap meet.
case.narrative:
On 03/24/2026 at approximately 1115 hours, AP associate Patricia Owens observed three male subjects enter Store #6652. The subjects split up: Subject 1 proceeded to the power tools aisle and began loading a flatbed cart with DeWalt combo kits (DCD999B, DCF903B) and Milwaukee M18 FUEL sets. Subject 2 grabbed a second cart and loaded Milwaukee packout tool boxes and a DeWalt table saw (DWE7491RS). Subject 3 positioned himself near the lumber yard exit.

At approximately 1128 hours, all three subjects converged at the lumber yard exit. Subject 3 held the gate open while Subjects 1 and 2 pushed the loaded carts through. They loaded merchandise into a dark blue Dodge Ram pickup truck backed up to the gate.

Owens activated the store's external cameras and obtained a clear plate: California 8XYZ123. DMV lookup returned the vehicle as registered to "Carlos Mendez" at a Colton address.

Owens contacted SBPD and provided footage. Reviewing prior incidents, this crew matches descriptions from HD stores in Rialto (03/10), Redlands (03/15), and Colton (03/19). Total combined loss from all four incidents: $22,600.

On 03/26, an informant reported that stolen power tools matching DeWalt/Milwaukee inventory were being sold at the Colton Indoor Swap Meet, vendor booth E-14. SBPD is coordinating a buy operation.

suspects.name: Carlos Antonio Mendez
suspects.clothing: Gray t-shirt, cargo shorts, black Nike Air Max. Tattoo sleeve on right arm.
suspects.other_desc: Vehicle registered owner. Tattoo sleeve is distinctive — tribal/geometric pattern from wrist to shoulder on right arm.
suspects.notes: Identified via plate lookup. Address: 1842 N Rancho Ave, Colton, CA 92324. Prior arrest for grand theft (2021), shoplifting (2019). Currently on probation in SB County.

suspects.name: Unknown Male 2
suspects.clothing: Red flannel shirt over white t-shirt, dark jeans, Timberland boots.
suspects.other_desc: Younger than Subject 1, estimated 20-25 years old. Small goatee.
suspects.notes: Handles the second cart. Moves quickly and appears to know exact product locations — possible former employee or repeated reconnaissance.

suspects.name: Unknown Male 3
suspects.clothing: Black tank top, basketball shorts, slides. Gold chain necklace.
suspects.other_desc: Gate man. Stands at lumber exit and waits. Has been at all four incidents in the same role.
suspects.notes: Always wears the same gold chain — visible in CCTV from all four locations. May be the crew leader based on observed coordination behavior.

vehicles.description: Crew cab, short bed, aftermarket tonneau cover. Tonneau cover used to conceal loaded merchandise. Chrome running boards.
vehicles.notes: Registered to Carlos Mendez, 1842 N Rancho Ave, Colton, CA 92324. Vehicle observed at all four incidents.

notes.note: Cross-referenced with Rialto HD (03/10): $4,200 loss, same MO. Rialto PD report #RPD-2026-5521. Their footage shows same Dodge Ram.
Redlands HD (03/15): $5,100 loss. AP associate recognized Subject 3's gold chain from TRACE network alert. Redlands PD report #RDPD-2026-1887.

Informant tip: stolen tools being sold at Colton Indoor Swap Meet, booth E-14. Vendor operating Thursday-Sunday. Product appears to match stolen inventory (DeWalt yellow packaging, some still with security tags).
SBPD detective assigned: Det. Frank Herrera, ORC Task Force, (909) 555-0199. Coordinating with Rialto and Redlands PD for joint operation. Buy-bust planned for 03/30 at swap meet.
Recovered $1,200 in DeWalt combo kits from Colton swap meet (undercover purchase). Serial numbers confirmed match to Store #6652 inventory. Evidence booked at SBPD.

evidence.description: CCTV — Subject 1 (Mendez) loading DeWalt combo kits onto flatbed cart, 03/24/2026 11:18
CCTV — All three subjects pushing carts through lumber yard exit, Subject 3 holding gate, 03/24 11:28
External camera — Dodge Ram plate 8XYZ123 backed up to lumber gate, clear plate capture
DMV record — Carlos Antonio Mendez, DL #M2294815, vehicle registration confirmation
Undercover photo of Colton Swap Meet booth E-14 — stolen DeWalt tools displayed for sale, some with security tags visible
SBPD Incident Report #SBPD-2026-28451, responding officer report
Serial number comparison — recovered swap meet items vs Store #6652 inventory loss records. 8 of 12 items confirmed match.
